title: Credential v1
description: A versioned envelope for credential metadata and references. Tracks credential identity, type, provider, source location, and lifecycle state. Producer: stipe, cortina, operator actions. Consumer: credential managers, auth bootstrap, deployment tools.

credential_id.description: Unique identifier for this credential. Examples: 'cred-anthropic-api-01', 'cred-aws-deploy-prod'.

credential_type.description: Type of credential. Determines how the credential is handled and refreshed.

provider.description: The service or system that issued this credential. Examples: 'anthropic', 'aws', 'google', 'github'.

scopes.description: List of scopes or permissions this credential grants. Examples: ['messages:write', 'read:user']. Empty array if not applicable.

source.description: Where the credential is stored. 'env' = environment variable, 'file' = file path, 'managed' = managed by external credential store.

source_ref.description: Reference to the source location. For 'env', the env var name. For 'file', the file path. For 'managed', a secret ARN or resource identifier. Null if not applicable.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of the credential.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the credential expires. Null if it does not expire.

refreshable.description: Whether this credential can be automatically refreshed (e.g., via refresh token).

last_used_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last successful use of this credential. Null if never used.

metadata.description: Optional metadata. May include 'key_id', 'region', 'project_id', 'note', or other producer-specific fields.
